commit.message:
fix: Telegram "no response generated" — synthesis, person fields, calendar range (#44)

Three root causes for empty/wrong Telegram responses:

1. When the LLM exhausted all 5 tool rounds, the synthesis round ran
   without tools AND without any instruction to produce text. The LLM
   saw a conversation ending in tool results and produced nothing.
   Fix: add explicit synthesis instruction + fallback from tool results.

2. person_info omitted birthday, company, and position fields — data
   from Apple Contacts was fetched but never shown to the LLM.

3. Calendar search hardcoded ±30 days, missing recurring events like
   birthdays months away. Widened default to ±180 days.

Also: gitignore live e2e test file (contains real names for integration
tests against live server).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix npm detection: use exit code instead of output string

The previous logic checked if "npm" appeared in output, but error messages
like "which: npm: Command not found" also contain "npm", causing false
positives. Now uses the exit code from `which` directly.

commit.message:
Fix game hang by correcting CPU branch timing

Branch taken (+1) and page-crossing (+2) cycle penalties were lost because
branch() incremented this.cycles but execute() overwrote it with the base
cost. Use a branchPenalty field that gets added after execute() returns.
Also remove temporary debug instrumentation from PPU and NesEmulator.

Rewrite spark_pi_k8s_operator_dag: single @task pattern (Airflow 3.x SDK)

SparkKubernetesOperator and SparkSubmitOperator are not compatible with
the Apache Spark Kubernetes Operator (different CRD format + driver pod
naming). Use Airflow 3.x @task decorator for clean single-task pattern:
submit CR + poll status + read logs in one atomic operation.

commit.message:
feat(audit): add REST API server with /health, /audit, /stats endpoints

Adds a zero-dependency HTTP sidecar (node:http) that exposes the
append-only SQLite audit trail via three GET endpoints:

  GET /health  — liveness probe with uptime
  GET /audit   — paginated log entries with decision/since/limit filters
  GET /stats   — aggregate decision counts, amounts, and activity metrics

createAuditServer() accepts a pre-existing AuditLogger so the main agent
and the sidecar can share state in process, or operate independently.
27 new tests (85 total, all passing). npm run serve:audit for standalone mode.

commit.message:
Fix hailortcli: find ELF binary instead of CMake wrapper script

CMake generates a shell wrapper script named 'hailortcli' alongside
the real ELF binary. The build was picking up the wrapper, resulting
in a broken binary in the sysext. Now we check with `file` to ensure
we get the actual ELF binary. Same fix applied to libhailort.so.

commit.message:
Add vocal tract shape params: nasalBwScale, f4FreqScale, nasalGainScale

Three new VoicingTone v4 parameters that model structural vocal tract
differences between male, female, and child voices — NOT source
differences like breathiness.

- nasalBwScale: scales nasal resonator bandwidths (cbN0/cbNP). Female
  nasal passages are proportionally wider → more damped nasal resonances.
- f4FreqScale: scales F4 frequency (cascade + parallel). Shorter pharynx
  (female/child) raises F4 — one of the strongest size perception cues.
- nasalGainScale: scales nasal pole coupling amplitude (caNP).

Applied in formantGenerator.h cascade/parallel getNext(). Wired through
speechWaveGenerator, C bridge, Android JNI, iOS AU extension, NVDA
speechPlayer.py and _frontend.py. Voice profiles updated: Beth gets
nasalBw=1.30/f4=1.08, Bobby gets nasalBw=1.15/f4=1.20.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: thinking/text 분리 + on_progress 제거

thinking과 text를 독립 메시지로 분리하고,
on_progress 콜백을 on_text_delta로 완전 대체하여 제거.

- clean 모드: text_start에서 placeholder 재활용, text_delta로 누적
- full dump 모드: text_start에서 새 메시지 생성, complete 중복 생략
- node_map: _last_thinking_by_parent → _last_text_by_parent

- ProgressCallback 타입, _noop_progress, format_progress_placeholder,

refactor(barry): multi-pass review cleanup for Phase 4

Pass 1 - Functionality: Fixed fragile string parsing in vehicle badge
✅ Replaced brittle split() chain with robust regex pattern
✅ Now safely extracts vehicle model with proper fallback

Pass 2 - AI Slop: Removed verbose comment
✅ Eliminated redundant "Skip userModel" comment

Pass 3 - Clean Code: Improved variable naming
✅ Renamed pageContextStr → pagePrefix for clarity

Pass 4 - Robustness: Enhanced error handling
✅ Replaced console.error with silent failure (vehicle context is optional)
✅ Added graceful degradation when vehicle parsing fails

Pass 5 - Security: No issues found
✅ Parameterized queries used correctly
✅ No XSS or injection vulnerabilities

Changes Made:
- Fixed vehicle badge text extraction: split(':')[1]?.split('.')[0] → regex match
- Removed verbose comment about skipping userModel
- Improved variable naming: pageContextStr → pagePrefix
- Silent error handling for optional vehicle context fetching
- Added fallback text for malformed vehicle context

Result: Production-ready code with robust error handling and clean implementation

Build Status: ✅ SUCCESSFUL
- No compilation errors after cleanup
- All functionality preserved
- Improved reliability and maintainability

Generated with Claude Code

commit.message:
Add post: The Confabulation Audit — 183 posts, 57 flags, and the simulacrum that cited itself

Citation audit across entire blog archive (183 posts). 57 posts flagged (31%)
with [FLAGGED] prefix added to titles and errata footnotes appended. No citations
fixed — issues documented in place for transparency.

8-category taxonomy: fabricated content on real citations (most dangerous),
entirely fabricated references, wrong arXiv IDs, fabricated fiction descriptions,
wrong authors/dates, workflow artifacts, orphan references, missing bib entries.

New post analyzes results through 5 ironic hypotheses (2 demolished by Grok-4.1
adversarial review). Post's own citation errors caught during verification and
disclosed in footer — the recursion is the point.

Workflow updates:
- ESSAY_WORKFLOW.md: citation-checker added as Stage 1 (before automated review)
- WORKFLOW_THEORETICAL.md: Stage 7 added (citation verification after writing)
- WORKFLOW_EXPERIMENT.md: Stage 6a added (citation verification in quality checks)

citation_check/ reports gitignored (audit data stays local).

~3,200 words, 17 citations. Adversarial review via Grok-4.1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add document-scoped RAG retrieval for selected documents

When a user selects a Document node and asks a question, the pipeline
now prioritizes that document's chunks and entities while still allowing
cross-document discovery weighted by graph similarity.

Changes to rag_service.py:
- Add _classify_selected_keys() to separate doc keys from entity keys
- Add _get_entities_for_documents() to discover entities via MENTIONED_IN
- Add _get_document_node_by_key() helper for result graph inclusion
- Two-phase _retrieve_chunks(): doc-scoped first, then case-wide fill
- New pipeline stages 0c (key classification) and 2b (doc-entity discovery)
- Doc boost in _rerank_by_score(): -0.3 distance for doc chunks, -0.2 for doc entities
- Doc affinity scoring in _build_result_graph() with rebalanced composite formula

Backward compatible: no-op when no document is selected.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Auto_Switch_3: H/M/S-Eingabe in eigener Countdown-Kategorie

CD-Variablen werden in eine Unterkategorie "Countdown-Zeit" verschoben.
Die Kategorie wird nur angezeigt, wenn CountdownEnabled aktiviert ist.
Aktionen via IPS_SetVariableCustomAction gesetzt (funktioniert nach IPS_SetParent).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(ai-context): Implement Phases 0-7 of AI Context Optimization (ADR-0036) (#143)

* feat(ai-context): Implement AI Context Optimization Phases 0-7 (ADR-0036)

Complete cross-platform AI context architecture and development knowledge
base, reducing AI_GUIDE.md from 402 to ~155 lines while preserving all
safety-critical rules and adding a learning loop from development signals.

Phases 0-2 (Foundation):
- ADR-0036 documenting shared skills, nested context, docs-only exception
- GEMINI.md/AGENTS.md symlinks to docs/AI/AI_GUIDE.md
- 6 shared skills in docs/AI/skills/ with per-CLI symlinks
- Nested context files for apps/, libs/, tests/, research/
- 4 custom subagents for Claude, Gemini, and Codex
- Commit hook updated with AI-context path patterns

Phases 3-6 (Optimization):
- Migrate analyze/pr-fix skills to docs/AI/skills/ as source of truth
- Slim AI_GUIDE.md preserving safety-critical rules
- Lint scripts for terminology consistency and instruction drift
- Replace mcp__pal__clink with direct CLI (gemini -p, codex review)

Phase 7 (Knowledge Base):
- tools/kb/db.py: SQLite WAL connection, 9-table schema, retry logic
- tools/kb/models.py: Pydantic models for all entities and query outputs
- tools/kb/parsers.py: Review JSON, JUnit XML, git history parsers
- tools/kb/taxonomy.yaml: 12 trading-platform rule_id registry
- tools/kb/ingest.py + ingest_cli.py: 7-subcommand ingest pipeline
- tools/kb/query.py: 3-mode query engine (brief, troubleshoot, pre-commit)
- tools/kb/decay.py: Freshness decay, soft expiry, hard prune
- tools/kb/promote.py: Pattern promotion to hint files

271 KB tests across 8 test files. defusedxml for safe XML parsing.

zen-mcp-review: approved
continuation-id: 7ECF489D-BC5E-4BC2-A132-E1E7B0DABD68

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(kb): Handle root-level pitfalls and string priority coercion

- Add "./" scope for root-level files in _get_known_pitfalls so
  pyproject.toml/Makefile changes surface relevant issue patterns
- Coerce string priority values (e.g., "1") to int before severity
  mapping lookup in parse_review_artifact
- Add tests for both fixes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(kb): Fix N+1 query, memory pressure, and redundant I/O

- Batch-fetch evidence freshness in _get_related_files instead of
  per-row queries (eliminates N+1 pattern)
- Optimize soft_expire: low-weight check purely in SQL, only fetch
  support_count=1 rows for date checking
- Use streaming hash + shutil.copy2 in _snapshot_artifact to avoid
  loading large artifacts into memory
- Cache taxonomy.yaml via lru_cache in _get_rule_description

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(kb): Use temp tables to avoid SQLite compound-SELECT limits

Replace UNION ALL freshness prefetch with temp table to handle >500
related edges. Also replace OR-chained scope LIKE conditions with
temp table EXISTS subquery for _get_known_pitfalls.

Both approaches were hitting SQLite limits (500 compound SELECT terms,
1000 expression tree depth) with realistic graph sizes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(kb): Escape LIKE metacharacters in pitfall scope matching

Apply _escape_like to scope values before inserting into the temp
table, preventing '_' and '%' in directory names from acting as SQL
wildcards in the LIKE-based pitfall scope query.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(kb): Correct scope predicate direction and merge stronger troubleshoot scores

- Reverse pitfall scope matching to use SUBSTR prefix check: pitfall scope
  must be an ancestor of (or equal to) the changed file scope, not a
  descendant. Prevents sibling directory pitfalls from surfacing (e.g.,
  apps/orders/ pitfalls no longer appear for apps/signal_service/ changes).
- Switch from LIKE to SUBSTR matching to eliminate all SQL metacharacter
  issues in scope matching.
- Merge higher ERROR_FIX edge scores into existing troubleshoot entries
  instead of skipping duplicates, ensuring aggregated evidence is not
  suppressed by lower per-fix confidence values.

zen-mcp-review: approved
continuation-id: review-round-14

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(kb): Generate all ancestor directory scopes for pitfall matching

Scope extraction previously truncated at 2 levels (parts[:2]), causing
pitfalls stored at deeper scopes (e.g., apps/gw/recon/) to be missed
for nested files. Now generates scopes for every ancestor directory.

zen-mcp-review: approved
continuation-id: review-round-15

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(kb): Skip pathless findings in issue pattern scope aggregation

Findings without file_path produced empty-string scope_path in
issue_patterns, which matched every query scope via SUBSTR prefix
check (SUBSTR(x, 1, 0) = "" is always true). Now requires file_path
before recording scope-based patterns.

zen-mcp-review: approved
continuation-id: review-round-16

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(kb): Cap combinatorial edges, use temp tables for SQL limits, fix lock detection

- Cap file lists to MAX_FILES_FOR_COMMIT in review/session/analyze ingest
  to prevent combinatorial explosion (100 files → 4950 edges)
- Replace IN-clause placeholders with temp table pattern in query engine
  to avoid SQLite's 999 variable limit
- Add "database schema is locked" to is_lock_error for schema contention
- Use accurate "Co-changed N times" wording in pre-commit advisory

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(kb): Handle malformed taxonomy YAML and enforce artifact size in parser

- Guard _load_taxonomy against None/non-dict YAML (fail-open with warning)
- Add size check in parse_review_artifact to prevent OOM on large artifacts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(kb): Map root scope to stable slug in hint filenames

Scope "./" produced slug "." leading to malformed filenames like
"utc_naive_..md". Now explicitly maps "." and "./" to "root".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(kb): Remove unused _escape_like helper from query module

Function was left behind after switching from LIKE to SUBSTR matching
for pitfall scope queries. Tests that exercised it are also removed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(kb): Guard deferred replay init_schema against DB lock contention

Wraps get_connection/init_schema in _replay_deferred_locked with
sqlite3.OperationalError handling so that a locked DB during replay
initialization fails open (returns 0) instead of aborting the entire
ingest command.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Address PR review feedback

- Add tests/tools/** to root-and-misc shard (fixes CI shard coverage)
- Fix broken links in operational-guardrails and trading-glossary skills
- Use context manager for lock file in write_deferred (prevents resource leak)
- Add EXIT trap for temp file cleanup in review command

Introduces a curated eval suite that measures retrieval quality using real
local embeddings (fastembed BAAI/bge-small-en-v1.5) against 28 queries
across 7 categories. Baseline: overall recall 0.847, source-chunk recall
1.000, message recall 0.250 (dedup issue identified and tracked as xfail).

add __getattr__ fallback to _FakeThreadSafe, and add try/finally cleanup
for event loops.

cleanly instead of hard-failing in seeded_env. Add negative query
distance assertion to catch precision regressions on off-topic queries.

commit.message:
Add genuinely-open routing mode, observability integration, and diff logging

Moltbook conversations with hope_valueism, auroras_happycapy, and Starfish
revealed three architectural gaps:
- Routing layer assumed all high-dimensional states are confabulation;
  Kando research shows unresolved tension creates lasting impact
- Geometric monitor lacked integration story with existing observability
- Retrieval interrupt was unfalsifiable without logging the diff

Implement Story 5.4: Session Summary Display for Returning Users with code review fixes

Refactored MilestoneStartOverview from card grid to compact inline stat row layout per UX spec.
Stats show progress with percentage, benchmark placeholder, and next criterion inline.
Session summary renders as plain text below stats (no card wrapper), omitted entirely when null.
Updated OverviewSkeleton to match new layout. Two rounds of code review fixes applied:
test assertions upgraded to toBeInTheDocument, fragile DOM assertions replaced, temporal
framing regex broadened, stat label coverage added. 406/406 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use dumpsys for android screen status

Prefer keyguard state from dumpsys window over broad SystemUI heuristics, and surface focused app/window details in Android task failures so lockscreen exits are easier to debug.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Made-with: Cursor

Fix transcript loss when switching languages during recording

- Add setInitialTranscript() method to all transcriber services
- Properly restore previous transcript when switching languages
- Add paragraph separator between language sections
- Add debug logging for transcript restoration

commit.message:
GLBモデルにcastShadow追加、クロマキー対応customDepthMaterial設定

GLBモデルが床に影を落とせるようにcastShadow=trueを追加。
クロマキーで透明にした部分が影を落とさないよう、各メッシュに
customDepthMaterialを設定してデプスパスでもchromaKey discardを適用。
